description: Serving as a means of pursuing an aim; important in achieving something.

description: Not enough to meet demand; inadequate; not sufficient for the purpose.

description: To overwhelm with things to be dealt with; to flood.

description: Not connected with or relevant to something; not applicable.

description: To cause a person or place to be alone or apart from others; to separate.

description: To show or prove that something is right or reasonable; to give good reason for.

description: To express regret or sadness about something; a passionate expression of grief.

description: More merciful or tolerant than expected; not strict or harsh in approach.

description: To use something to maximum advantage; the power to influence people or events.

description: Responsible by law; likely to do or to be affected by something.

description: To feel intense dislike or disgust for; to hate greatly.

description: Of imposing height; noble or ambitious in nature; excessively high-minded.

description: Long life; the long duration of an individual's life or of something's existence.

description: To be hidden and waiting for a purpose; to read discussions without contributing.

description: To make something appear larger than it is; to increase in significance or effect.

description: An official authorization or instruction to carry out a policy or task.

description: Clear and obvious; showing plainly; a list of passengers or cargo.

description: To treat someone as if they are unimportant; to push to the edge of relevance.

description: To follow a winding course; to wander without a definite direction or purpose.

description: To interfere in something that is not one's concern; to intrude.

description: Having a feeling of pensive sadness; inclined to melancholy.

description: An experienced and trusted adviser; to advise and train someone less experienced.

description: In an orderly and systematic way; done according to a clear plan or process.

description: To cause someone to have a wrong idea about something; to deceive.

description: To lessen the gravity, seriousness, or painfulness of something; to moderate.

description: Average in amount, intensity, or degree; not extreme; to control or regulate.

description: Of great importance or significance, especially in having a bearing on future events.

description: A distinctive recurring feature or theme in artistic, literary, or musical work.

description: To confuse someone; to bring into a disordered or confused state.

description: Showing a lack of experience, wisdom, or judgement; innocently simple.

description: Having an excessive interest in or admiration of oneself and one's appearance.

description: To plan and direct a route; to deal successfully with complex situations.

description: So small or unimportant as to be not worth considering; insignificant.

description: Existing in name only; not real or actual; very small; relating to a name.

description: In a casually calm manner that shows a lack of interest or concern.

description: Worthy of attention or notice; remarkable; a famous or important person.

description: A concept or belief; an impulse or desire, especially one of a whimsical kind.

description: Harmful, poisonous, or very unpleasant; injurious to health.

description: Characterized by subtle shades of meaning or expression; finely detailed.

description: To care for and encourage the growth or development of something.

description: Extremely unpleasant or offensive; objectionable in a persistent way.

description: To keep from being seen; to make unclear or unknown; not well known.

description: To preoccupy or fill the mind of someone continually; to fixate on.

description: To counterbalance or compensate for; a consideration that counteracts something.

description: To leave out or exclude; to fail to include or mention.

description: Involving an amount of effort or difficulty that is oppressively burdensome.

description: Hopeful and confident about the future; expecting the best outcome.

description: To be heavier, greater, or more significant than; to exceed in value or importance.

description: Failed to notice or consider; not given sufficient attention.

description: To succeed in dealing with a problem or difficulty; to defeat or prevail over.

description: To state something too strongly; to exaggerate the importance or truth of.

description: To calm an angry or violent person or situation; to bring peace to.

description: With great care and thoroughness; in a very careful and detailed way.

description: More important than anything else; supreme in importance or authority.

description: A strong supporter of a party or cause; prejudiced in favor of one side.

description: Treating someone as if they are less intelligent or less capable than oneself.

description: A strong liking or tendency toward something; a particular inclination.

description: To spread through or be present throughout; to penetrate and pass through.

description: Continued effort to do or achieve something despite difficulty or failure.

description: Tending to see the worst aspect of things or believe the worst will happen.

description: In a way that seems reasonable or probable; believably.

description: To make an emotional appeal; to present a case in court; to state formally.

description: Existing in or yielding great quantities; more than adequate; abundant.

description: Division into two sharply contrasting groups or sets of opinions.

description: Having great power, influence, or effect; strong or powerful.

description: In a practical way, dealing with problems as they actually exist.

description: To come before something in time, order, or position.

description: A very steep rock face or cliff; a situation of great danger.

description: Marked by exactness and accuracy of expression or detail; clearly defined.

description: Present as the strongest or main element; most common; having greatest influence.

description: Occurring or done before the usual or proper time; too early.

description: Based on reasonable probability; giving grounds for a particular inference.

description: Most common or widespread in a given area at a given time; current.

description: Acting in accordance with morality and showing recognition of right and wrong.

description: To designate or treat something as more important than other things.

description: Very great or intense; having or showing great knowledge and deep understanding.

description: Happening or developing gradually or in stages; favouring change and reform.

description: To formally forbid something by law, rule, or other authority.

description: Producing many works, results, or offspring; highly productive.

description: To spread and promote an idea, belief, or information widely.

description: Corresponding in size or degree; having the correct or required proportional relationship.

description: Expected or likely to happen in the future; potential.

description: To stimulate or give rise to a reaction or emotion; to deliberately anger or annoy.

description: In a careful and sensible manner that avoids unnecessary risks.

description: To appear or claim to be or do something, especially falsely.

description: To put an end to a rebellion or other disorder; to suppress or subdue.

description: Representing the most perfect or typical example of a quality or class.

description: Advocating complete political or social reform; relating to the root of something.

description: A kingdom; a field or domain of activity, interest, or knowledge.

description: To reject in an abrupt or ungracious manner; a sharp rejection.

description: To establish by calculation; to consider or regard in a specified way.

description: A source of help in a difficult situation; the right to demand payment from someone.

description: The state of being superfluous; the condition of being no longer needed.

description: To frame or present something in a new or different way; to alter the context.

description: To assign to a lower position or less important role; to demote.

description: Unwillingness or disinclination to do something; hesitancy.

description: A solution to a problem; a medicine or treatment for a disease or illness.

description: An unintended consequence occurring after an event or action; a rebound effect.

description: A large supply of something; a natural or artificial lake for storing water.

description: Remaining after most of something has gone or been taken; a quantity left over.

description: To prevent from doing something; to keep under control; to limit or restrict.

description: To make an attack or take action in return for a similar action; to seek revenge.

description: A surprising and previously unknown fact, especially one that is made known suddenly.

description: Language designed to have a persuasive or impressive effect; the art of effective speech.

description: Extremely thorough, careful, and accurate; exacting in terms of standards.

description: A person or thing competing with another; to be equal to or surpass another.

description: Strong and effective in all conditions; sturdy and unlikely to fail.

description: Disastrously damaging or harmful; causing ruin or great financial loss.

description: Most noticeable or important; prominent; standing out from the rest.
example: The most salient finding in the report was largely ignored in the media coverage.

description: To examine or inspect closely and thoroughly; to observe with great care.

description: Not connected with religious or spiritual matters; relating to worldly affairs.

description: A view of or attitude toward a situation or event; a feeling or emotion.
example: Public sentiment shifted rapidly once the full details of the scandal emerged.

description: Having sharp practical judgment; clever in a businesslike or self-interested way.

description: A person inclined to question or doubt accepted opinions; one who questions claims.

description: Unity or agreement of feeling or action, especially among individuals with common goals.

description: The forming of a theory without firm evidence; investment in risky ventures.

description: The state of not flowing or moving; a period of little or no growth or development.

description: In a resolutely firm and unwavering manner; without being deflected.

description: To raise levels of physiological or nervous activity; to encourage activity or interest.

description: Seeking or intended to undermine or destabilize an established system.

description: The combination of ideas, elements, or substances to form a new whole.

description: Fanatical and uncompromising pursuit of ideals; excessive zeal.
example: Political zealotry on both sides prevented any productive discussion from taking place.
